channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US LNG Exporters Poised for Windfall Profits Amid Iran Conflict and Qatar LNG Disruptions
item.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran, involving US and Israeli strikes, has severely disrupted global energy markets, particularly by forcing the closure of Qatar's massive Ras Laffan LNG plant, which account...

item.title: Chinese Espionage and Its Strategic Influence on the Philippines Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has entered its second week, with US and Israeli AI-guided airstrikes against Iran and Lebanon showing no clear end in sight. The Philippines faces indirect eco...

item.title: Carlton sidelines Sam Docherty amid voice memo controversy; Brisbane to appeal player bans
item.description: Carlton Football Club has removed former co-captain Sam Docherty from a scheduled speaking role at a corporate event following the release of a profanity-filled voice memo in which he criticised the t...

item.title: IndiGo Delhi–Manchester Flight Returns Mid-Air Due to Middle East Airspace Restrictions
item.description: An IndiGo flight 6E 033 operating from Delhi to Manchester had to return to Delhi shortly after take-off due to sudden airspace restrictions linked to the escalating tensions in the Middle East. The a...

item.title: Five Namibian Cheetah Cubs Born at Kuno National Park, India’s Cheetah Population Reaches 53
item.description: On 9th March 2026, a significant event occurred at Kuno National Park as Jwala, a Namibian cheetah, gave birth to five healthy cubs. This event has increased the total cheetah population in India to 5...

item.title: KP government announces two-month fuel conservation plan, cuts vehicle fuel allowance
item.description: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government has announced a set of fuel conservation measures for two months amid the global fuel crisis caused by the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran. The provincial cabinet, led ...

item.title: Koala sets IPO valuation at $260 million ahead of institutional and retail offers
item.description: Koala, the online furniture retailer, has set the price for its initial public offering (IPO), reaching an enterprise valuation of $260 million. The valuation reflects a multiple of 10.5-times the com...

item.title: Health Minister Confirms Sanitary Pads Remain Safe After Chemical Study
item.description: Health Minister Dr Aaron Motsoaledi has reassured women and girls across South Africa that it is safe to continue using sanitary products, despite recent research detecting small amounts of chemicals ...

item.title: Paddington The Musical wins nine awards at WhatsOnStage Awards, equalling record for new musical
item.description: Paddington The Musical has made theatre history by securing nine awards at the 2026 WhatsOnStage Awards, held at the London Palladium and voted for by the public. This tally equals the joint record pr...

item.title: Tanzania allocates 8.1bn/- to repair roads and bridges in Serengeti after heavy rains
item.description: Tanzania government is set to spend about 8.1 billion shillings to repair roads and bridges in Serengeti National Park damaged by heavy rains recently. The Natural Resources and Tourism Minister, Dr. ...

item.title: IRIS Lavan Iranian Ship Docked at Kochi Amid West Asia Conflict, India Extends Humanitarian Shelter
item.description: The Iranian naval ship IRIS Lavan continues to remain docked at Kochi port in Kerala, with its crew accommodated at Indian naval facilities, External Affairs Minister Dr S. Jaishankar informed the Raj...

item.title: Ex-Army General Must Refund N4 Billion After Court Upholds Conviction
item.description: The Court of Appeal for Abuja don confirm the conviction of Umar Mohammed, former Group Managing Director of Nigerian Army Properties Limited and dismissed major-general. Mohammed originally face tria...

item.title: Liberal Democrats urge cancellation of King Charles's planned state visit to the US amid tensions over Iran's conflict
item.description: The Liberal Democrats, led by Sir Ed Davey, have called on Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to advise King Charles III to cancel a planned state visit to the United States scheduled for late April. Thi...

item.title: PMD Monitors Possible Pollution from Iran Strikes Affecting Western Pakistan
item.description: KARACHI: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a warning that winds carrying pollutants from Iran may affect western parts of Pakistan following recent attacks on Iranian oil facilit...

item.title: FBI Interview Reveals Alleged Comments by Prison Guard on Epstein's Death
item.description: An inmate at New York City's Metropolitan Correctional Center has told the FBI that on the morning of Jeffrey Epstein's death, he overheard a prison guard comment, 'You killed that dude,' in relation ...

item.title: Phil Gould comments on Daly Cherry-Evans’ challenges after Roosters debut
item.description: During the recent NRL match, veteran commentator and former coach Phil Gould highlighted that Daly Cherry-Evans is facing a reality check after moving to the Sydney Roosters. According to Gould, the g...

item.title: PNP boosts police presence in markets and groceries to curb hoarding and profiteering amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has ramped up its deployment of personnel in local markets, groceries, supermarkets, and commercial areas nationwide. This move aims to stop any possible hoarding ...

item.title: Glasgow Central Station Closed Following Fire: Travel Disruptions and Ticket Advice
item.description: Travellers in Scotland are facing significant rail disruption after a fire broke out in a vape shop adjacent to Glasgow Central station on Sunday. The station, Scotland’s busiest, remains closed until...

item.title: Managing Social and Environmental Impacts of Mineral Sand Mining in Senegal
item.description: Senegal has been producing various minerals like zircon, ilmenite, and rutile for many years. In 2007, the government granted EGC (formerly GCO) a 25-year license to mine zircon across a large coastal...

item.title: Canadian woman loses $37,000 in tuition scam involving stolen credit cards
item.description: A Vancouver woman, Mira Burgess, fell victim to a sophisticated tuition scam, losing over $37,000 in fraudulent charges to her credit and debit cards. Scammers promise discounted tuition to internatio...
